Container.Name: seedsync
Container.Overview:
SeedSync syncs files from a remote Linux server (seedbox) to your local machine using LFTP.
Features: - Fast parallel downloads via LFTP - Web UI dashboard with real-time progress - Auto-queue for hands-free syncing - Archive extraction (RAR, 7z, zip) - Auto-delete from remote after download - Staging directory support (download to SSD, move to array) - Dark mode
Setup: After starting the container, open the Web UI and configure your remote server address, username, password/SSH key, and remote path in Settings.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the SeedSync web interface.

Config.Name: Config Path
Config.Description: Stores SeedSync configuration and state files.

Config.Name: Downloads Path
Config.Description: Where downloaded files are saved. Point this to your media share or downloads folder.

Config.Name: Staging Path
Config.Description: Optional staging directory. Enable staging in SeedSync Settings to download here first (e.g. fast SSD cache), then move files to the Downloads path. Leave mapped even if staging is disabled.

Config.Name: SSH Key (optional)
Config.Description: Path to your SSH private key for password-less authentication to the remote server. Leave empty to use password auth.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID for file permissions. Default 99 is the Unraid 'nobody' user.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID for file permissions. Default 100 is the Unraid 'users' group.

Config.Name: UMASK
Config.Description: File creation mask for downloaded files. Leave empty to use the default (0002 → rw-rw-r--). Common values: 000 (rw-rw-rw-), 022 (rw-r--r--).
